title: Hieracium juvonis
intro: Hieracium juvonis, commonly known as hawkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium juvonis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with hawkweed include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.

Hieracium juvonis is also sometimes referred to as a synonym for Hieracium pilosella subsp. juvonis.
uses: Hieracium juvonis is traditionally used in some folk medicine practices, particularly in Eastern Europe, where it's valued for its anti-inflammatory properties and potential as a treatment for various health conditions. The plant is believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which may aid in wound healing and skin infections. Additionally, Hieracium juvonis has been used to treat digestive issues and as a natural diuretic. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential side effects. Further studies are required to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
